title: Surface tool_use_description in every tool-call header
task:
Every tool call carries a `tool_use_description` envelope field — the model's brief plain-English statement of intent (system_prompt/sections.rs:58: 'Every tool call requires a tool_use_description field — briefly state what you are doing with this call and why'). The TUI's activity-log sidebar uses it; the scroll-region tool-call headers throw it away. Wire it in: the description renders ABOVE the existing tool header as a single dim line, so the user reads what the agent is trying to do BEFORE seeing the tool mechanics.

OUTCOME — the user sees:
  \x1b[2m'reading the config to check the timeout default'\x1b[22m  ← dim description line
  │ read  > /path/to/config.toml  (0.42s)              ← existing tool header

The description is single-line (truncated to 120 bytes if longer, same truncate_preview as command headers), rendered dim, and uses NO box-bar — copy-pasting it gives you the bare sentence. When the model omits tool_use_description (empty / absent), the description line is omitted entirely — the existing header is the only output, as today.

requirements.description: In write_tool_result (crates/norn-tui/src/app/dispatch.rs around line 530), extract the description via the existing extract_tool_use_description helper from app/helpers.rs (line 289). It takes the raw arguments JSON string. The dispatch path already calls extract_tool_use_description for the activity log (lines 76, 132); store the same value on PendingToolCall when handle_tool_call_complete runs (line 367) so write_tool_result can read it from state.pending_tools without re-parsing args.
requirements.acceptance: PendingToolCall struct carries the description String (or Option<String>). When ToolCallComplete fires, the description is captured. When ToolResult fires and write_tool_result runs, the description is available alongside args + output.

requirements.description: Prepend the description line to the tool header output. Format: `\x1b[2m{description}\x1b[22m\n` followed by the existing `│ {tool_name}  {header}` line. The description line has NO leading `│ ` bar — it sits flush with the left margin so the eye reads it as a separate human-scale statement, not a tool-mechanics row. When description is empty or None, the line is omitted (current behaviour preserved).
requirements.acceptance: Unit test under app/dispatch.rs::tests: a synthesised tool call with tool_use_description='checking the config' produces output where the FIRST line is the dim description string and the SECOND line begins with `│ {tool_name}`. A second test with empty/missing description verifies the description line is absent and the header is the only output.

requirements.description: Apply truncation cap of 120 bytes (HEADER_PREVIEW_MAX_BYTES already exists in tools/helpers.rs) so a model emitting a paragraph doesn't blow up the scroll region. Use truncate_preview from helpers.rs.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: a tool_use_description of 200 bytes is truncated to ≤120 bytes with a trailing `…` glyph.

requirements.description: Gates pass: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files returns zero.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/dispatch.rs — write_tool_result (line 530), handle_tool_call_complete (line 367), PendingToolCall struct (search up for it).
- crates/norn-tui/src/app/helpers.rs — extract_tool_use_description (line 289), truncate_preview is in tools/helpers.rs (not app/).
- crates/norn-tui/src/tools/helpers.rs — truncate_preview (line 72), HEADER_PREVIEW_MAX_BYTES (line 17).
- crates/norn/src/system_prompt/sections.rs — TOOL_ENVELOPE_GUIDANCE describes the field's contract.
- crates/norn/src/loop/tool_dispatch.rs — build_envelope splits tool_use_description from model_args (line 289).

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — don't reinvent:
- extract_tool_use_description helper. Use it.
- truncate_preview helper for the 120-byte cap.
- The activity-log path already passes the description through correctly — that path doesn't need touching.

DESIGN TASTE: the description is the human-readable layer; the `│ tool ...` line is the mechanics layer. Dim makes 'this is context, not output.' No bar on the description because it's a sentence, not a tool row. One blank-ish line of visual separation is enough.

STANDARDS: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; files under 500 LOC.
